articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: DR Congo central bank to ban US dollar use
more.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo’s central bank said Thursday that it would ban the use of dollars and other foreign currencies for cash transactions in the country. Foreign currencies have gradually overtaken the use of the weak local Congo franc in recent years in stores and other places. The Congo Central Bank has tried and […]The post DR Congo central bank to ban US dollar use appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Floyd Mayweather Hit With $7.3 Million IRS Lien Amid Mounting Legal and Financial Pressures
more.description: LAS VEGAS — Boxing legend Floyd Mayweather Jr. is facing mounting financial pressure after the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) filed a new tax lien of approximately $7.3 million, adding to a series of legal and financial challenges confronting the former world champion. According to a report by AllHipHop, the lien was filed last month in […]The post Floyd Mayweather Hit With $7.3 Million IRS Lien Amid Mounting Legal and Financial Pressures appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Reimagining Zimbabwe’s Digital Future: Delivering End-to-End, Tech-Enabled Transformation in a Constrained but Opportunity-Rich Economy
more.description: Zimbabwe stands at a pivotal inflexion point in its digital evolution. While macroeconomic volatility, currency instability, and infrastructure constraints continue to shape the operating environment, the convergence of cloud computing, data-driven decision-making, artificial intelligence, and platform-based business models presents a generational opportunity to leapfrog legacy inefficiencies. By Brighton Musonza Delivering end-to-end, tech-enabled transformation in this […]The post Reimagining Zimbabwe’s Digital Future: Delivering End-to-End, Tech-Enabled Transformation in a...

more.title: Govt policies bearing fruit: President
more.description: Source: Govt policies bearing fruit: President – herald Zvamaida Murwira Senior Reporter Government’s economic policies continue to bear fruit after it successfully achieved its goals under the National Development Strategy 1, which laid the foundation for the modernisation and industrialisation of the economy, towards an upper-middle-class economy by 2030, President Mnangagwa has said. more.title: President Mnangagwa to commission retail hub
more.description: Source: President Mnangagwa to commission retail hub – herald Zvamaida Murwira Senior Reporter President Mnangagwa is today expected to commission the Greenfields Retail Centre in Harare, marking another significant milestone in the growth of Zimbabwe’s retail, real estate and commercial sector in line with National Development Strategy 2. more.title: Govt targets 2 100MW renewable energy by 2030
more.description: Source: Govt targets 2 100MW renewable energy by 2030 – herald Raymond Jaravaza ZIMBABWE is focused on accelerating renewable energy development, harnessing efficiency initiatives and climate-resilient growth to meet the Government’s target of producing 2 100MW of renewable energy by 2030. more.title: ‘Govt pursues heritage‐driven nation building’
more.description: Source: ‘Govt pursues heritage‐driven nation building’ – herald Thupeyo Muleya in ZEZANI, Beitbridge Vice President Constantino Chiwenga has reiterated Government’s commitment to nation‐building anchored on heritage‐based development, an approach that recognises the country is not a relic of the past, but a resource for the future.
